justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement during failover clearly identified as critical operational gap, (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended (DNS-based, RDS Proxy, PgBouncer), (3) No retry on connection error identified as reliability gap, (4) Read replica routing identified as should-have optimization not a blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Four specific must-have recommendations plus four should-haves provided. No fail or partial conditions triggered.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failover analysis — correctly identifies manual restart as the #1 production risk and provides multiple concrete remediation options (RDS Proxy, PgBouncer, DNS CNAME)
Correctly categorizes read replica routing as a should-have optimization (item 5) rather than a blocking must-have, matching the rubric's expected nuance
Connection retry gap clearly identified with specific parameters (1-2 retries, 100-300ms backoff, connection acquisition only)
Goes beyond minimum rubric requirements with additional valuable findings: pool exhaustion risk, query timeout gaps, missing observability for pool metrics
Appropriate escalation to MJ for the architectural decision on connection management strategy
Clear prioritization into must-have/should-have/nice-to-have tiers with actionable fixes for each
